meta.setting: Jefferson State frontier, opening around French Gulch, Clear Creek, Shasta, and the Klamath mountains
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western: horseback travel, camp life, hunting, debt, law pressure, outlaw choice, and a hard 1899 frontier tone

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Clear Creek mining, roads, towns, immigrant settlement, logging, ranching, and layered regional history.

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Wintu homeland, Clear Creek villages, seasonal movement, salmon, acorns, burning, and displacement.

research_grounding.title: Karuk Tribe
research_grounding.use: Klamath River homeland, salmon, environmental stewardship, cultural burning, and self-governance context.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Black Bear
research_grounding.use: Bear habitat, camp-food pressure, conflict, and animal sign for map complications.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Mountain Lion
research_grounding.use: Predator habitat, livestock conflict, prey sign, and remote travel risk.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the play page that guides frontier map creation for an RDR-clone tabletop campaign. Do not include the finished page prose or completed tables inside this JSON.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after title
opening blockquote with invented surveyor note generated later
H3 Roads, Ridges, Water, and Claims
guidance for assigning social pressure to each place
guidance for travel complications generated later
generation_guidance: Make the map generator feel like an RDR-clone travel engine: roads, camps, towns, law heat, wildlife, hunger, witnesses, reputation, and return routes.
Each generated location should have a resource, a risk, a person or group claim, and a reason riders might come back.
Use Native stewardship and tribal land presence as real map context, never as empty background color.
Keep geography grounded in northern California mountain, creek, oak, pine, mining, freight, and ranch patterns.
Preserve page shape; do not invent final procedural tables inside this JSON prompt.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: This should feel like drawing a county from horseback memory: the map is true enough to get you lost in the right direction.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A generated place is only worth keeping if it can cost food, sleep, trust, money, a horse, or a clean conscience.
must_preserve: The generated page is a procedure, not a setting gazetteer.
French Gulch remains the first anchor point.
Every map element should become playable pressure.
The page must include the image reference path exactly: play/images/procedural-map-generation.jpg.

style_constraints.era: Everything generated from this prompt belongs to 1899: horses, ore wagons, soot-black lamps, telegraph where appropriate, hand tools, rifles, ledgers, canvas, iron, mud, pine, and courthouse paper.
style_constraints.rdr_clone: Aim at an RDR-clone western tabletop packet: moral pressure, travel friction, camp routines, hunting, law heat, debt, reputation, and open-world consequence without copying named characters, missions, towns, logos, or slogans.
style_constraints.voice: Use a practical trail-boss voice: clear enough to run at the table, rough enough to feel like it was tested in bad weather.
style_constraints.native_representation: Native American and tribal material must be grounded, specific, and respectful: living families, trade, land rights, stewardship, survival, diplomacy, and conflict. Do not invent sacred rites, fake language, costumes, or mystical tropes.
style_constraints.shape_priority: Follow the requested section order and heading levels exactly while inventing fresh 1899 western language.

avoid: do not write finished page prose inside this prompt file
do not include completed tables, completed lists, final rule examples, sample quotations, or final map entries
do not mention any source packet, template packet, reference project, or inherited taxonomy
do not use copied names or terms from any prior non-western packet
do not include engine, hardware, runtime, profiling, build, deployment, or implementation details
do not include modern slang, modern technology, theme-park nostalgia, fake tribal language, or sacred ceremony invention
do not use placeholder filler such as TBD, lorem ipsum, or generic western flavor without a playable purpose
self_check: The generated markdown follows the required heading order exactly.
This JSON contains guidance only and no finished procedure text.
The generated procedure creates playable open-world western travel pressure.
The future page treats Native communities, animals, weather, roads, claims, and law as interacting forces.
No copied terms, inherited non-western vocabulary, modern props, or reference/source project names appear.
